news_overview: Iran escalated Strait of Hormuz attacks Jul 7-10 (LNG tanker Al Rekayat and supertanker Wedyan struck; 3 ships hit in 24h), unraveling the June MOU; Hormuz traffic largely blocked since Feb 2026; crude-tanker war-risk surcharges jumped 25%->37%.
pick_overview: Large-cap pure VLCC crude-tanker owner; spot day-rates spike on rerouting, reduced effective fleet supply and war-risk premia.
rationale: Contested Hormuz forces ton-mile expansion (longer routes) and strands tonnage, tightening the global crude tanker market; Frontline's VLCC fleet earns directly off spot TCE spikes. Second-order beneficiary of the geopolitical shock, not the oil price itself.
exit_plan: Trim 1/3 at +15%, 1/3 at +30%; exit remainder on Hormuz de-escalation/ceasefire headline or -12% stop.

news_overview: USDA Jul 10 crop report: smallest US wheat harvest since 1972 (HRW smallest since 1957/58) from Plains drought; corn stressed in pollination; Hormuz closure spiking global natural gas and fertilizer costs.
pick_overview: US Gulf-Coast nitrogen (ammonia/urea/UAN) producer running on cheap Henry Hub gas.
rationale: Global gas spike from Hormuz lifts world urea/ammonia prices (much global N uses expensive imported gas) while CF's feedstock is cheap US gas, widening margins; drought keeps N demand firm for corn. Second-order: a foreign energy shock benefits CF via feedstock arbitrage, not merely 'fertilizer price up'.
exit_plan: Trim 1/3 at +12%, 1/3 at +25%; exit if Henry Hub spikes above ~$5.50 erasing the spread or -12% stop.

news_overview: SK Hynix's record $26.5B Nasdaq debut (Jul 10, +13%); NVIDIA-SK hynix multiyear HBM memory partnership; Samsung/SK $1T Korea semis/AI investment; Korean team demos stacking 10+ ultrathin chips (advanced packaging).
pick_overview: Advanced-packaging inspection & metrology equipment maker levered to HBM/3D stacking.
rationale: HBM and 3D/hybrid-bond stacking multiply inspection/metrology steps per wafer; capex flows to packaging tools, not just memory makers. Camtek is a focused second-order supplier riding HBM unit growth without single-customer memory-price risk.
exit_plan: Trim 1/3 at +15%, 1/3 at +30%; exit on HBM capex guidance cut or -15% stop.

news_overview: ~15 reactors (~12 GW) online globally in 2026; uranium seen breaching $70/lb; record US power demand; energy-security push to reshore enrichment; nuclear generation growth accelerating.
pick_overview: Only US-owned uranium enrichment company; HALEU producer for advanced/SMR reactors under DOE contracts.
rationale: The nuclear-buildout bottleneck is enrichment (still dominated by Russia's Tenex), not just mined uranium; security-driven reshoring routes funding and offtake to domestic enrichment. Second-order pick vs crowded uranium miners.
exit_plan: Trim 1/3 at +20%, 1/3 at +40%; exit on loss of DOE HALEU funding/contracts or -15% stop.

news_overview: EIA sees record US power demand in 2026 & 2027; AI data-center buildout bottlenecked by 'electricity, copper and critical gases'; grid equipment granted 15% tariff protection through 2027 to speed domestic buildout.
pick_overview: Maker of custom electrical switchgear, distribution & control systems for data centers, LNG, grid and industrial power.
rationale: Every data center, LNG train and grid expansion needs medium-voltage switchgear with long lead times and pricing power. Smaller-cap second-order beneficiary of the AI-power theme, less crowded than turbines/utilities.
exit_plan: Trim 1/3 at +15%, 1/3 at +30%; exit on backlog/book-to-bill rollover or -15% stop.

news_overview: NATO Ankara summit (Jul 6-7): tens of billions in new deals; European allies +20% defense spend; multinational buys (Triton drones, GlobalEye, A330 MRTT); 5 allies to hit 3.5% GDP core-defense guideline in 2026.
pick_overview: Mid-cap defense supplier in unmanned systems, target drones, hypersonics and propulsion/subsystems.
rationale: Broad Western rearmament lifts sub-tier suppliers of drones/munitions-enablers/propulsion faster than saturated primes. Kratos is a second-order, higher-beta beneficiary (affordable mass, attritable drones) rather than the named prime contracts.
exit_plan: Trim 1/3 at +20%, 1/3 at +40%; exit on continuing-resolution/budget stall or -15% stop.

news_overview: China halted sulfuric-acid exports to Chile (fully cut off by May 2026), the key input for Chile's copper heap-leach operations, compounding a global sulfur bottleneck traced to the Iran war. Chile copper output fell 9.04% YoY in March 2026 (Codelco -10%, BHP Escondida -15.75%, Collahuasi -10.8%). Morgan Stanley forecasts a 600,000-tonne 2026 refined copper deficit -- the largest in 20+ years.
pick_overview: Mid-cap copper/gold/zinc miner (Peru, Manitoba, Arizona) with no material Chile heap-leach/sulfuric-acid exposure.
rationale: This is a chemical-input failure, not an ore-grade problem, so it disproportionately hits Chile-concentrated leach producers while sparing concentrate/flotation producers elsewhere. As the deficit widens, non-Chile supply gains relative pricing power. Second-order: not "copper price up" but "a Chile-specific input-chain failure reallocates market share/margin to non-Chile producers."
exit_plan: Trim 1/3 at +15%, 1/3 at +30%; exit remainder if Chinese sulfuric-acid exports to Chile resume, or -15% stop.

news_overview: QatarEnergy invoked force majeure on LNG shipments; Iranian strikes damaged ~17% of Qatar's Ras Laffan export capacity with a 3-5 year repair timeline. Renewed strikes this week reignited transit fears; European gas prices jumped ~5% on Jul 13. Europe is racing to refill storage before winter, with the US now supplying 63% of EU LNG imports (up from 57%).
pick_overview: FSRU (floating storage regasification unit) operator expanding European LNG import/regas capacity (Germany-Wilhelmshaven, Italy-Ravenna; ~6 new/expanded EU terminals forecast for 2026).
rationale: Qatar's multi-year capacity loss plus persistent Hormuz risk means Europe must lock in MORE import/regas capacity to receive LNG from alternative sources -- an infrastructure bottleneck distinct from the tanker freight-rate story already used for crude (FRO). Excelerate's floating terminals deploy faster than fixed onshore plants, capturing the urgency premium.
exit_plan: Trim 1/3 at +18%, 1/3 at +35%; exit remainder on confirmed Ras Laffan capacity restoration, or -15% stop.

news_overview: Brook Mine (Ranchester, WY) officially opened Jul 11 -- the first new US rare earth mine in 70+ years, DOE/NETL-backed, ~1.7M tons of rare earth oxides from unconventional coal-associated clay that avoids costly hard-rock processing. G7 leaders agreed to cut reliance on any single non-G7 rare-earth supplier below 60% by 2030; China broadened export controls on US/Japanese entities this month.
pick_overview: US metallurgical coal producer whose Brook Mine subsidiary is developing the newly-opened Wyoming rare-earth project.
rationale: G7 diversification targets and tightening Chinese controls create policy-level urgency and funding support for non-China rare-earth supply. Ramaco's unconventional, low-radioactivity clay deposit sidesteps the capital intensity that kills most hard-rock rare-earth projects -- differentiated from the already-crowded MP Materials/Lynas trade. CAVEAT (why only Medium, not High): core coal segment posted a Q1 2026 net loss and negative adjusted EBITDA, and the rare-earth project is still pilot/PEA-stage -- a longer-dated call option, not near-term revenue.
exit_plan: Trim 1/3 at +20%, 1/3 at +40%; exit remainder on DOE funding withdrawal/negative PEA update, or -18% stop (wider given coal-segment volatility).

news_overview: Pentagon aims to more than triple annual Patriot PAC-3 production (600 to 2,000/year); $9.0B Saudi Patriot sale and ~$5B Qatar Patriot/APKWS restock already approved; batteries redeployed from South Korea/Germany to the Middle East. This week's Iran strikes plus Houthi/Iran attacks on Jordan, Saudi Arabia and Kuwait underscore the demand driver.
pick_overview: Aerospace/defense subsystem supplier (payload protection/deployment, aerostructures, propulsion/interstage systems) for hypersonics, strategic missile defense and tactical missile programs -- not a named prime.
rationale: The Patriot/missile-defense surge is a manufacturing-capacity story as much as a demand story; the physical bottleneck runs through sub-tier structural/propulsion suppliers who fulfill the expanded build rate across MULTIPLE missile programs. Karman's active pipeline grew to ~$3B (from ~$1B a year ago) with revenue +51% YoY. NOTE: shares a sector (defense) with existing open pick KTOS -- different specific program driver (Patriot/missile-defense production ramp vs. NATO/European rearmament), flagged as a concentration risk.
exit_plan: Trim 1/3 at +20%, 1/3 at +40%; exit remainder on Patriot funding cut/CR stall, or -15% stop.
